prerequisites: A StockData.org account. Sign up for free at stockdata.org to gain access.
An API token, found on your StockData dashboard after signing up. This maps to the connector's required api_key field.
A start date for the data window (start_date config field), which corresponds to the API's published_after date filter (accepted formats include Y-m-d and Y-m-d\TH:i:s).

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: StockData authenticates with a single API token passed as the api_token query parameter on every request. Provide it in the connector's api_key secret field. Obtain the token from your StockData dashboard after creating a free account.

setupSteps.title: Create a StockData account and get your API token
setupSteps.body: Sign up for a free account at stockdata.org. After signing up, open your dashboard to find your API token. This token is added to every API request as the api_token GET parameter.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API token
setupSteps.body: Enter your StockData API token in the required api_key field. The connector sends it as the api_token query parameter to authenticate.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest point you want to pull data from. This corresponds to the API's published_after filter and accepts formats such as Y-m-d (for example 2024-01-01) or Y-m-d\TH:i:s.

setupSteps.title: Optionally narrow the data with filters
setupSteps.body: Set symbols to a comma-separated list of ticker symbols (for example AAPL,TSLA,MSFT) to limit results to specific instruments. Set industries to a comma-separated list (for example Technology,Industrials) to filter by industry. Set filter_entities to true to return only the entities relevant to your query with each article. All three fields are optional.
